core_payload.content:
# Agent Evals and Traces

## 1. Definition and Essence
Agent Evals and Traces defines the feedback layer that records what an agent did, why it did it, and whether the result was good enough. Traces preserve run lineage. Evals score or classify outcomes so the system can learn which patterns are useful, wasteful, unstable, or unsafe.

## 2. Core Responsibilities
- `run_traces` record handoffs, tool use, compact decisions, and outcome lineage across a swarm cycle.
- `eval_loops` score plans, reviews, capsule jobs, and execution outputs against explicit quality criteria.
- `replay_and_diff` lets operators compare runs and inspect what changed when a lane regressed or improved.
- `review_gates` give reviewer and validation lanes structured evidence before Dream or Real promotion.
- `failure_clustering` groups repeated breakdowns such as malformed JSON, timeout churn, or low-value duplicate jobs.
- `assignment_lineage` keeps traces attached to assignment handles, lane identity, and branch targets instead of generic run blur.
- `canary_tasks` provide a fixed set of representative tasks to test model or workflow changes before broad rollout.
- `activity_projection` sends key trace and eval outcomes into control-plane and operator views instead of burying them in raw logs.

## 3. N1Hub Role
In N1Hub this pattern belongs inside Background Agent Runtime, Vault Stewardship Swarm, Symphony observability, and the Dream-to-Real review path. A swarm that produces plans without traces cannot be debugged. A swarm that executes jobs without evals cannot become calmer or sharper over time. The same trace layer should also tell the control plane which lane worked, what branch it touched, and why the system currently trusts or distrusts the result.

## 4. Architectural Value
Evals and traces turn autonomy from a vibe into an inspectable engineering loop. They make cost, quality, failure, improvement, and governance visible at the same time.

## 5. Operational Rule
If a lane changes behavior but the system cannot replay, compare, and score that change, the lane is still operating as folklore.

## 6. Philosophical Alignment
Agent Evals and Traces supports To Dig Deep by requiring deep work to leave behind evidence, lineage, and critique rather than only outputs.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Agent Evals and Traces defines the feedback and lineage layer for N1Hub agents. It combines run traces, structured eval loops, replayable artifacts, review gates, assignment lineage, failure clustering, and canary tasks so swarm work can be inspected and improved over time. This matters because autonomous work without traces cannot be debugged, and autonomous work without evals cannot become more trustworthy or more efficient. In N1Hub, this pattern is what turns repeated swarm cycles into an engineering loop instead of folklore about which agent felt smart.
